page: Microsoft Azure DC32es_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEV6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture, utilizing an Intel processor. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, which provides a memory allocation of 4.0 GB per core.

The DC32es_v6 instance does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external Premium Storage options, which the instance is capable of supporting. There are no integrated GPUs present on this server, and it does not come with any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With dedicated CPU allocation and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers predictable compute resources for enterprise workloads. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which represents a configuration tradeoff to consider when planning deployment costs. This server is designed for workloads such as database management, enterprise applications, and general-purpose business-critical systems that require dedicated CPU resources and Premium Storage compatibility.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32es_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEV6 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for external disk attachments. It also lacks integrated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Positioned as a memory-optimized and premium-storage-capable instance, the DC32es_v6 provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with dedicated CPU allocation. This configuration is suitable for enterprise applications, database workloads, and general-purpose business systems requiring consistent compute performance without local storage dependencies.
